rationale: The citing paper uses Noseworthy and Trudel (2011) in core problem framing and theory development, not just as a passing background mention. It is cited to support the claim that design communication failures occur when consumers cannot infer what a product does, and again in the intrinsic/function sections to justify that consumers infer function from form and that form-function assessment shapes design evaluations. These points feed directly into the DCAS conceptualization of form and function as key dimensions.
relation_description: The citing paper treats the cited paper as prior empirical grounding for its central form-function communication logic, especially the idea that product form influences perceived functionality and evaluation. This evidence helps justify why DCAS must assess whether design communications make function understandable.
